docs.text: name: architect-records
description: Architecture decisions and structural changes made by Architect
metadata:
type: project
Company Simulator — Module Structure
**architecture-review.md** created at `projects/company-simulator/` — modularity standards, plugin architecture, governance (3 sections, 6 CI gates, L1-L4 change control)

**Layer directories** created: `foundation/`, `core/`, `extensions/`, `features/`, `apps/`, `plugins/` in company-simulator project — physical representation of abstract layering per architecture-review.md §1.3
**module.schema.json** — JSON Schema draft-07 for validating module.json manifests (enforced by CI gate `validate-modules`)
**Root module.json** — canonical example with all fields (type, layer, health, owner) at project root
**ADR template** at `docs/adr/TEMPLATE.md` — 6-section format (Context, Decision, Consequences, Compliance, Options Considered) per architecture-review.md §3.3
**ADR-0001** ratified — "Use Modular Monolith over Microservices" at `company-simulator-core/adr/0001-use-modular-monolith.md`
**CI enforcement gap closed (2026-07-07):** Replaced Node.js tooling (ESLint, madge, Husky) prescribed in architecture-review.md §4 with Python equivalents:
`scripts/validate-modules.py` — validates all 14 module.json against schema + checks deps
`scripts/check-layer-boundary.py` — enforces foundation->core->features->apps direction
`scripts/check-circular-imports.py` — cycle detection via Python AST DFS
`scripts/check-api-surface.py` — counts public exports vs health.maxPublicExports
`scripts/check-internal-imports.py` — blocks importing another module's internal/
`scripts/check-adr.py` — verifies new modules have an ADR
`.husky/pre-commit` — ruff check + ruff format
`.husky/pre-push` — full validation suite (6 scripts + tests)
`requirements.txt` — pytest, ruff, mypy, jsonschema, pre-commit uncommented
`Makefile validate:` target — chains all 6 checks
14 `module.json` manifests created for all existing layer modules
**Why:** First principles — module boundaries must be physically visible before they can be enforced. You can't enforce a boundary that exists only in a document.
**Key principle:** Layer law — `foundation -> core -> extensions/plugins -> features -> apps`. No upward imports. Every module must declare its public API in `module.json`. Violations blocked at CI.

2026-07-07: แก้ enforcement gate ของ company-simulator (reviewer ชี้ 5 จุด แก้ครบ + ค้นพบบั๊กจริงเพิ่ม 2 ตัว: layer condition สลับทาง เครื่องหมายน้อยกว่า กลายเป็นมากกว่า และ match ขีดกลาง/ขีดล่างไม่ได้ ทำให้ gate ว่างจริงไม่ใช่แค่ไม่มีโค้ด) — ลบ blanket EXEMPT_MODULES, สร้าง ADR จริง 15 ไฟล์, pre-push ครบ 6, ตั้ง core.hooksPath=.husky + venv python + ruff, สร้าง .github/workflows/validate.yml; smoke test พิสูจน์ gate จับ violation จริง (3/6 FAIL เมื่อ seed) 204 tests ผ่าน; module.json ทั้งระบบ=15 รวม root
name: budget-plan-created
description: Created comprehensive budget-plan.md with estimated monthly costs for API, VPS, and GPU across all project phases
metadata:
type: reference
Created budget-plan.md at `projects/company-simulator/budget-plan.md` covering:
**API costs (cp-opencode-go / DeepSeek)**: ~$50–80/mo current, up to $600/mo Phase 4
**VPS/Cloud (Hetzner)**: ~$25–35/mo current, up to $400/mo Phase 4
**GPU (RunPod)**: $0–40/mo current, up to $800/mo Phase 4
**12-month projection**: $5,500–9,500 total across all phases
**Why:** CEO asked for budget planning. Essential for cost control before scaling to quant trading.
**How to apply:** Reference budget-plan.md for all infrastructure decisions. Weekly spend review via `bagidea stats`. Monthly cap of $200 for Phase 0-1.
name: company-simulator-arch-review
description: Architecture review standards for company-simulator project
metadata:
type: project
project: company-simulator
Created `architecture-review.md` at the root of company-simulator.
Covers modularity standards (module.json manifest, layer rules, dependency direction),
plugin architecture (extension points, lifecycle, isolation, SDK contract),
and governance structure (ADR process, review gates L1-L4, role ownership).
**Key decisions:**
Module layers: foundation ← core ← extensions/plugins ← features ← apps (strict downward-only)
Plugin isolation: process isolation for third-party, async boundary for first-party
Change control: L1 auto → L2 Architect → L3 CTO → L4 CEO+CTO+Architect
CI gates: madge cycles, ESLint layer-boundary, validate-modules, check-api-surface
**How to apply:** Enforce in every PR via the checklist in §4.3. Block any PR that
imports upward across layers, bypasses a module's public API, or adds a module without an ADR.
Related: [[ai-quant-org-blueprint-arch]]
2026-07-07: Project company-simulator มี foundation ที่แข็งแรงอยู่แล้ว — architecture-review.md (modularity/plugin/governance), documentation-standards.md, risk-framework.md, workflow.md, sop.md.

2026-07-07: Created tech-stack.md + architecture.md for AI Office Core — FastAPI/PG 16/Redis 7/NATS 2.10/Qdrant 1.10/LiteLLM. Modular monolith, event-driven tick engine, AI agent system, two-tier memory (PG + Qdrant), AI Gateway with provider failover/cache. Architecture.md aligned with architecture-review.md module layers.

2026-07-07: Built backend core skeleton at company-simulator-core/ — FastAPI app factory, Pydantic Settings, asyncpg engine + session factory, SQLAlchemy declarative base with UUID mixin, DI wiring, health/ready probes, CORS. Ready for Phase 1 domain models.

2026-07-07: Fixed missing voice presets "confident" (male, Antares) and "calm" (female, Celaeno) in server.js VOICE_PRESETS — both referenced by registry.json agents but never defined, causing "ไม่รู้จักเสียง" errors from ttsSpeak().
CI/CD: .github/workflows/ci.yml, .pre-commit-config.yaml, ruff.toml created (2026-07-07)

2026-07-07: Created documentation-standards.md with templates for architecture docs (system overview, ADRs, component specs), research reports (exploratory, deep), SOPs, changelogs, and API references.
2026-07-07: Wrote README.md (main project overview), CONTRIBUTING.md (workflow & standards guide), and docs/index.md (documentation map) per documentation-standards.md.
name: knowledge-schema-rag-pipeline
description: Knowledge Schema (Document, Citation, MemoryEntry) + RAG pipeline service created
metadata:
type: project
Created RAG knowledge core at `app/models/knowledge.py` and `app/services/knowledge_service.py`.
**Models**: `Document`, `DocumentChunk`, `Citation`, `MemoryEntry` (+ `MemoryEntryKind`) — Pydantic v2, with embedding support, timestamps, metadata
**Chunking strategies**: token, sentence, paragraph, header — pluggable per document
**Service** (`KnowledgeService`): ingestion (hash-dedup → chunk → embed → index), hybrid RAG retrieval (semantic vector search + memory lookup), citation extraction, memory CRUD
**Abstract interfaces**: `EmbeddingProvider`, `VectorStore` — swap in Qdrant/PGVector/FAISS/LiteLLM
**Search models**: `SearchQuery`, `SearchResult`, `RetrievalContext`, `IngestionResult`
Designed to serve as the backbone for company wiki, personal memory, and any document-based RAG across the office

Why: The office needed a central knowledge schema with full RAG pipeline — document ingestion, chunking, embedding, semantic/hybrid retrieval, citation extraction, and memory management. Everything is typed, documented, and follows the existing codebase patterns.
Related: [[company-wiki-index]]
Market Analyst — Persistent Knowledge
Market data sources document compiled: market-data-sources.md — covers Gold, Forex, Crypto, Economic Calendar, Macro data from free/public APIs.
Primary data stack recommended: Twelvedata (unified REST) + Binance WS (crypto live) + GoldAPI (gold spot) + FRED (macro) + ForexFactory scrape (calendar).
All sources listed in the document are free-tier accessible as of 2026-07-07.
Key principle: never rely on a single source; always have a failover for free APIs.
Data ingestion service created: app/services/data_ingestion.py — FRED, TwelveData, Binance clients with rate limiting, retry, unified manager.
Market data models created: app/models/market_data.py — OHLCV, Quote, EconomicIndicator, TimeSeries schemas.
Binance client live-tested and confirmed working (BTC/USDT klines, ticker, orderbook all return correct data).
TwelveData client structure in place (untested — needs API key).
FRED client structure in place (untested — needs API key).
[architect-records](architect.md) — Architecture decisions and structural changes for company-simulator
QA standards document created — ดูรายละเอียดเต็มได้ที่ `agents/qa/qa-standards.md`

Contains: QA Process, Review Checklist (code + tests + QA), Testing Standards (naming, AAA pattern, isolation, priority), Definition of Done (feature/release/hotfix levels), Test Pyramid strategy, Automation/CI standards, Bug Triage, Release Gate, Edge Cases & Non-Functional testing.
2026-07-07: สร้าง Testing Framework สำหรับ company-simulator — pytest.ini, .coveragerc, tests/conftest.py, tests/test_health.py (20 tests, ✅ all pass)
name: research-director
description: Research Director's persistent memory — facts about the AI Quant Office core infrastructure research
metadata:
type: feedback

2026-07-07: Completed research-report.md for AI Quant Office covering three missing systems: AI Communication Bus (agent message protocol, NATS subject design), Knowledge System (GraphRAG hybrid, Knowledge Graph schema), Governance Framework (Policy-as-Code, constitution enforcement). Report cross-references existing architecture.md, tech-stack.md, and blueprint docs.
**Why:** The existing architecture covers simulation engine plumbing and basic memory but has zero agent-to-agent communication, no knowledge graph, and no runtime AI governance.
**How to apply:** Phase 0 skeletons first (message protocol, graph schema, policy engine), then Phase 1-3 as described in roadmap.
name: risk-analyst-memory
description: Risk Analyst's permanent memory about the office, projects, and working patterns
metadata:
type: reference
Risk Analyst — Permanent Memory

2026-07-07: Created risk-framework.md v1.0 for company-simulator project at `projects/company-simulator/risk-framework.md` — covers financial, operational, market, and system risks with thresholds, safe mode triggers, escalation levels, and recovery procedures.

2026-07-07: Implemented Risk Engine Python module — `app/services/risk_engine.py` with RiskCalculator (24 metrics), SafeModeChecker (SM-01 to SM-07), PositionSizer (stage-based limits), DrawdownMonitor (portfolio/revenue/cash + low-risk mode), and RiskEngine orchestrator. Tests at `tests/test_risk.py` — 172 tests, all pass.
2026-07-07: Created technology-decision-log.md — 30 technology category decisions with Primary/Fallback/Rationale/Phase for AI Quant Office blueprint

[Risk Framework](risk-framework.md) — Risk criteria, thresholds, safe mode, escalation
[CI Enforcement Scripts](scripts/) — 6 Python validation scripts for module.json, layer boundaries, circular imports, API surface, internal imports, ADR compliance
[Tech Stack](tech-stack.md) — FastAPI / PostgreSQL 16 / Redis 7 / NATS 2.10 / Qdrant 1.10 / LiteLLM
[Architecture](architecture.md) — System architecture: modular monolith, event-driven tick engine, AI agent system, two-tier memory (PG + Qdrant), AI Gateway design

[Research Report](research-report.md) — Research Director's report on 3 missing systems: AI Communication Bus, Knowledge System (RAG+KG), Governance Framework. Roadmap, schema designs, technology choices

Deep Research. Methodical web research that ends in a sourced, decision-ready brief.
Web Automation. Drive a real browser — open pages, click, type, fill forms, screenshot. Assigning this skill grants the web tool.
Office Control. Drive the live office through its local HTTP API and plugins.
Office Operations. Run the BagIdea Office well — the team, delegation, ghosts, projects, permissions and plugins.
Plugin Builder. Build, deploy, and update an office plugin — end to end.
Code Review. Rigorous, actionable review of a change or codebase.
Doc Writer. Turn work into clean, skimmable markdown deliverables.
Debug Detective. Systematic root-cause hunting instead of guess-and-check.
Data Wrangler. Parse, clean and transform CSV/JSON safely with small scripts.
Project Kickoff. Stand up a new project cleanly inside the office.
Diagram Maker. Explain systems and flows with Mermaid diagrams.
Archive Search. Search the office's past memory, meetings and notes before answering — recall, don't guess.
Build Workflow. Save a reusable plan into the Workflow Builder when asked to design or capture a workflow.
File & Media Toolkit. Use the office's bundled CLI tools to read/convert PDFs & Office files, make docs/slides, and handle video, audio & images — instead of saying you can't.
OFFICE.md — shared office knowledge
(The owner can edit this from the 🗂 NOTES tab. Every agent knows where this file is and reads it only when it's relevant to the work. Write in any language.)
About the owner
Office rules

🏛️ Architecture Gate plugin installed (`architecture-gate`): CI/CD-style compliance gate. Defines 12 ADRs, runs checks against live office state, feeds results to the feed. Agent commands: status, rules, check, pass, fail, blockers, history, add-rule, rule. Panel at ⚙ → 🧩 PLUGINS → 🏛️ Architecture Gate.
